civ-accordion.html:
Veterans who served on active duty for at least 24 continuous months, or the full period for which they were called or ordered to active duty, may be eligible for VA health care.

Bring the following when you apply:

  • DD-214 (separation papers)
  • Social Security number
  • Most recent tax return
  • Insurance card, if you have one

Most applications are processed within 7 business days. You'll receive a written notice of your enrollment decision in the mail.

The Department of Veterans Affairs collects information from you to determine your eligibility for VA benefits and services.

How we use your information

  • Verify your eligibility for benefits
  • Process your claims and payments
  • Provide medical care and services
  • Communicate with you about your benefits

When we share your information

We share your information only with authorized federal partners, and only when required to deliver your benefits or comply with the law.

By using VA.gov, you agree to the terms of service. The terms cover acceptable use, your rights, and our obligations.

        @civ-change=${(e: Event) => {
          const target = e.target as HTMLElement;
          const checked = (target as any).checked;
          const children = target
            .closest('fieldset')!
            .querySelectorAll<any>('civ-checkbox:not([name="select-all"])');
          children.forEach((cb: any) => {
            cb.checked = checked;

civ-data-grid.name: Keyboard grid navigation (role=
civ-data-grid.html:
With keyboardNav, the table promotes to role="grid" and becomes a single tab stop. Once focused, use:

  • Tab — focus the grid; Tab again leaves it.
  • ← → ↑ ↓ — move between cells.
  • Home / End — first / last cell in the current row.
  • Ctrl+Home / Ctrl+End — first / last cell in the grid.
  • PageUp / PageDown — jump 10 rows.
  • Enter / Space — activate the cell (sort, toggle checkbox, open menu, start edit).
  • F2 — start edit on editable cells (Excel convention).
  • Esc — cancel an active edit.

<civ-fieldset legend="Government-issued ID">
      <civ-select label="ID type" name="idType" required>

          <option value="drivers-license">Driver's license</option>
          <option value="state-id">State ID card</option>
          <option value="passport">U.S. Passport</option>
          <option value="military-id">Military ID (CAC)</option>

      <civ-conditional when="idType" has-value>
        <civ-file-upload
          name="idFront"
          accept="image/*"
          capture="environment"
          hint="Take a photo or upload an image of the front"
          required
        ></civ-file-upload>

        <civ-conditional when="idType" not-equals="passport">
          <civ-file-upload
            name="idBack"
            accept="image/*"
            capture="environment"
            hint="Take a photo or upload an image of the back"
            required

      </civ-conditional>
    </civ-fieldset>

<civ-file-upload label="Upload documents (simulated)" hint="Add files to see simulated upload progress. Some uploads will randomly fail to demonstrate error handling and retry." name="simulation"
          multiple
          max-files="5"
          @civ-change="${simulate}"
          @civ-upload-retry="${(e: Event) => {
            const el = (e.target as HTMLElement).closest('civ-file-upload') as CivFileUpload;
            const detail = (e as CustomEvent).detail;
            if (!el || detail?.index === undefined) return;
            const fileIndex = detail.index;
            el.setFileStatus(fileIndex, 'uploading', { progress: 0 });
            let progress = 0;
            const interval = setInterval(() => {
              progress += Math.floor(Math.random() * 20) + 10;
              if (progress >= 100) {
                progress = 100;
                clearInterval(interval);
                el.setFileStatus(fileIndex, 'success');
              } else {
                el.setFileStatus(fileIndex, 'uploading', { progress });
              }
            }, 250);
          }}"
        ></civ-file-upload>

civ-form.name: Prefill: Remote Data Source

<!--
      When prefill-src is set, civ-form fetches JSON from that URL on connect.
      While loading, a "Loading your information..." status message is shown.
      If the fetch fails, an error message with a retry link appears.

      The JSON response must match PrefillData shape:
      {
        "name": { "value": "Jane Doe", "source": "profile" },
        "email": { "value": "jane@agency.gov", "source": "profile", "locked": true }
      }

      Events:
      - civ-prefill-applied: fired after fields are populated
      - civ-prefill-error: fired if the fetch fails

      In this demo, the URL won't resolve, so the error state is shown.
    -->
    <civ-form
      form-label="Prefill from API"
      prefill-src="data:application/json,{}"
      @civ-prefill-applied="${(e: CustomEvent) => {
        console.log('Prefill applied:', e.detail);

civ-process-list.name: Benefit application — three-step preview
civ-process-list.html:
You'll need your Social Security number, proof of income, and a government-issued photo ID.

Most people finish in about 15 minutes. You can save and come back later if you need to.

A decision usually arrives within 30 days. You'll get a letter in the mail with the result.

civ-process-list.html:

We'll send a confirmation link to the address on file.

PDF, JPG, or PNG, up to 10 MB per file.

Double-check everything before sending.

We confirmed your identity using ID.me.

You're here. Complete the form below to file your claim.

Within 30 days after submission.

civ-process-list.name: Rich body — nested lists and links
civ-process-list.html:
You'll need the following:

  • Your Social Security number
  • Your most recent W-2 or pay stub
  • Bank routing and account numbers (for direct deposit)

The form takes about 20 minutes. Use the save-and-continue button if you need a break.

After submission, you can check the status of your application in your account dashboard.

civ-read-more.name: Custom labels
civ-read-more.html:
A dependent is a spouse, child, or parent who relies on you for financial support.

More specifically: a spouse you are legally married to; an unmarried child under 18 (or under 23 if enrolled full-time in school); a permanently incapacitated child of any age whose disability began before age 18; or a parent whose income falls below the federally set threshold.

You may be eligible for a benefit if you meet certain service and discharge criteria. Most benefits are available to veterans who served on active duty for a minimum length of time and received a discharge under honorable conditions. Some benefits also extend to members of the Reserves and National Guard who completed a period of active service, and to surviving family members of veterans who died in service or from a service-connected condition.

Specifically, you must have served at least 24 months of active duty and been discharged under conditions other than dishonorable. Some benefits also require a documented service-connected disability.

If you separated before the 24-month requirement due to a service-connected disability or a hardship discharge, you may still be eligible. Contact a Veterans Service Officer to confirm.

civ-read-more.name: Expanded by default
civ-read-more.html:
Today is the last day of open enrollment.

Your selections must be submitted by 11:59 PM Eastern. Late submissions cannot be accepted; you will need to wait until the next enrollment window in November.

civ-read-more.name: Inline (last words of the paragraph)
civ-read-more.html: We use the income information you provide to determine your benefit amount and verify eligibility. Specifically, gross monthly income is compared against the income limit for your household size; net worth is also reviewed against the applicable cap.

civ-read-more.name: No fade trigger (plain button below text)
civ-read-more.html:
You may be eligible for a benefit if you meet certain service and discharge criteria.

Specifically, you must have served at least 24 months of active duty and been discharged under conditions other than dishonorable. Some benefits also require a documented service-connected disability.

civ-read-more.name: Graceful with no rest content
civ-read-more.html: This is the entire message. The "Read more" button still renders but clicking it reveals an empty region — useful when you author the rest content conditionally and don't yet have something to show.

civ-read-more.name: On a colored background (override --civ-read-more-bg)
civ-read-more.html:
The fade defaults to white. On a colored card, override --civ-read-more-bg with the card's fill so the gradient resolves to the surrounding color instead of fading to white.

Without the override, the gradient would fade from the page text into a white band — visually disconnected from the colored card it sits in. With the override matched to the card color, the trigger reads as part of the same surface.

civ-read-more.name: Small size
civ-read-more.html:
Your application has been received and is in review.

The average review time is 3–5 business days. You will receive an email with the decision and any next steps.

civ-read-more.name: With chevron icon
civ-read-more.html:
We use the income information you provide to determine your benefit amount and verify eligibility.

Specifically, gross monthly income is compared against the income limit for your household size. Net worth (assets minus debts, excluding your primary residence and one vehicle) is also reviewed against the applicable cap.

civ-relationship.name: Deceased Assumed vs Show Deceased
civ-relationship.html:
✓ Good — deceased-assumed

The form is a survivor benefit claim, so the context already establishes that the Veteran died. Skip "Is this person deceased?" and ask for the date directly.

✗ Avoid — show-deceased

Making a grieving family member confirm "Yes, this person is deceased" before they can enter the date is insensitive and repeats information the form already knows.

civ-text-input.name: Below-input action slot (escape hatch)
civ-text-input.html:
{ const host = (e.currentTarget as HTMLElement).closest('civ-text-input'); if (host && 'value' in host) { navigator.clipboard?.writeText(String(host.value ?? '')); } }} >

Use the data-below-action slot for value-shortcuts the design system doesn't ship as first-class props (copy, paste, scan, generate). The slot renders in a helper row directly under the input — a larger, clearer tap target than an inset button inside the input's chrome.

For ephemeral confirmation, drop in <civ-confirm-button>. It handles the "Copy → Copied ✓ → Copy" feedback loop, keeps padding stable across the swap, and wires aria-live="polite" so screen readers announce the receipt. Consumer does the actual work in the civ-confirm listener.

The inset action region is reserved for one control: the close / × clear button. Anything else — Copy, Today, Now, Show / Hide password, custom shortcuts — belongs in the below-input helper row.

civ-text-input.name: Inset: Password reveal
civ-text-input.html:
Click the Show password / Hide password button to toggle visibility. The rendered input type flips between password and text; the host's type prop stays password. Suppressed for non-password types.

The Show / Hide toggle sits in a helper row below the input, not inset. The only affordance that stays inset is the × clear button. Reveal and clear can both be set together — clear stays on the trailing edge inside the input, reveal lives below.

civ-toggle.name: Description vs Hint
Reduced motion (preview)
civ-toggle.html:
When the user's OS reports prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, CivUI sets all transition durations to 0.01ms globally. The same controls still update — they just don't animate. This story simulates the override locally.

To verify against the real media query: macOS Accessibility → Display → Reduce motion, Windows Visual effects → Animation effects off, or Chrome DevTools → Rendering → Emulate prefers-reduced-motion: reduce.
